title: AprilTag Vision for Robot Pose Estimation
subtitle: AprilTags give a robot field-relative visual landmarks that can correct drifting odometry.
summary: The robotics season used PhotonVision, AprilTags, multiple cameras, and pose estimates to update drivetrain odometry.

artifact.title: Pose correction enters the drivetrain world model
artifact.description: The swerve vector demo represents the motion side; AprilTags add external field landmarks that correct pose estimates.

contentBlocks.text: Wheel encoders and gyros estimate motion by integrating small changes. Small errors accumulate. A camera seeing a known AprilTag can provide an absolute field reference and pull the estimate back toward reality.

contentBlocks.content:
Detect tagFind corners and decode the tag ID.
Know field poseLook up where that tag lives on the field.
Solve camera poseEstimate where the camera must be.
Fuse estimateAdd the vision measurement to pose estimation with uncertainty.

sections.heading: From Tag to Robot Pose
body: The tag ID tells the system which field landmark is visible. The observed tag corners plus camera calibration estimate the camera's pose relative to the tag. The robot-to-camera transform then converts camera pose into robot pose.
The estimate should carry uncertainty. A faraway tag, motion blur, partial occlusion, or bad calibration should count less than a close clean observation.

sections.heading: Multi-Camera Systems
body: Multiple cameras widen field coverage and reduce blind spots, but they add calibration and synchronization complexity. Each camera needs its own transform relative to the robot frame.
A robust system rejects impossible estimates, handles latency, and avoids over-trusting vision during collisions or ambiguous tag layouts.

rows: More tags usually improve pose confidence
Some tag poses have multiple plausible solutions
Old measurements must be inserted at the correct time
Bad camera intrinsics or transforms corrupt every estimate

pitfalls: Adding every vision estimate with equal trust.
Using vision during high-motion blur without filtering.
Assuming odometry and vision coordinate systems match automatically.

quiz.question: What does an AprilTag ID provide?
quiz.explanation: The ID lets the robot look up the tag's known field pose.
